headline: 467 airframes grounded across 25 states with $50M–$2B national replacement exposure as propeller supply shows 96% China dependency and FPV camera stock reveals 97% critical component concentration risk.

lead_story.title: NASAO Survey Confirms 467 Airframes Grounded Nationally
lead_story.body: Oregon NASAO Revision 2 white paper (February 2026) reports 467 drone airframes grounded or restricted across 23 states based on survey of 25 state DOTs. National replacement exposure ranges from $50M to $2B depending on procurement pathway and replacement timeline. This is the first quantified national impact assessment of FCC Covered List and NDAA Section 1709 enforcement at the state operational level. The grounding affects active fleet inventory already deployed for infrastructure inspection, emergency response, and survey missions.
lead_story.action: Convene program stakeholders this week to audit current fleet against FCC Covered List entities (DJI, Autel, Anzu, SkyRover, Cogito/SPECTA) and develop a 90-day replacement timeline with itemized budget scenarios between $50M–$2B based on agency-specific exposure.

supply_chain.signal: 463 of 484 tracked propeller models (96%) are Chinese-manufactured, dominated by HQProp, Gemfan, and DAL (all Shenzhen/Guangdong). Propellers are not considered NDAA critical components under current interpretation, creating a compliance gap.
supply_chain.window: Immediate risk — no domestic alternative exists at scale; compliance interpretation could shift within one legislative cycle (6-12 months).
supply_chain.action: Issue RFI to domestic composite manufacturers for propeller production feasibility study; budget 15-20% cost premium for non-Chinese sourcing if available; stockpile 18-month supply of current propellers as bridge inventory.

supply_chain.signal: 187 of 193 tracked FPV cameras (97%) are Chinese-manufactured. FPV cameras ARE NDAA critical components (imaging hardware with data storage/transmission capability), making current supply base non-compliant for federal procurement.
supply_chain.window: 60-90 days — existing inventory may be depleted before compliant alternatives scale; Q3 2026 budget cycles will require documented compliance.
supply_chain.action: Halt new FPV camera procurement from Chinese sources immediately; identify and qualify the 6 non-Chinese camera models in Forge database; engage with U.S. or allied manufacturers for emergency production contracts with 120-day delivery target.

supply_chain.signal: 212 of 303 tracked motors (70%) are Chinese-manufactured, led by T-Motor/Tiger Motors (62 models, Nan'an City, Fujian), iFlight, and BrotherHobby. 30% non-Chinese supply exists but capacity and lead times unknown.
supply_chain.window: 90-180 days — motor procurement cycles are longer than cameras/props; Q4 2026 builds will need locked supply.
supply_chain.action: Map the 91 non-Chinese motor models by performance class and availability; issue dual-source contracts splitting orders 70/30 between established non-Chinese suppliers and domestic development partners; accept 6-month lead time extension for non-Chinese motors.

supply_chain.signal: GUR and Ukrainian military intelligence confirmed RPi 4B as core component of 'Borscht Tracker V3' in Geran-2 (Shahed-136) kamikaze drones. 40,000+ units estimated diverted to Russian defense production through third-country transshipment.
supply_chain.window: Immediate regulatory risk — export control tightening likely within 30-60 days; procurement may face enhanced end-use certification requirements.
supply_chain.action: Document full supply chain custody for all RPi 4B units in current inventory; prepare enhanced end-use certifications for future orders; evaluate alternative SBC platforms (NVIDIA Jetson Orin Nano, Lattepanda) to reduce dependency on controlled components.

gray_zone.status: Texas AG filed 7-count DTPA lawsuit February 18, 2026 in Collin County; no Anzu response filed as of April 4 (45+ days frozen); TX seeking injunctive relief and civil penalties up to $10K/violation.
gray_zone.development: Teardown analysis confirms PCB layouts align exactly with DJI Mavic 3 Enterprise, with matching board part numbers. Controller RRC01 is relabeled DJI hardware. Firmware is signed and encrypted with DJI cryptographic keys, meaning DJI retains root key control despite Anzu's U.S. branding. Product discontinuation predicted at 91% confidence by Q2 2026 due to DJI P1 OCU chipset embargo enforcement.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: Any federal, state, or local agency that purchased Anzu Raptor platform under belief it was non-DJI hardware faces compliance failure under NDAA Section 1709 and FCC Covered List rules. Estimated exposure includes agencies in all 50 states that sought 'Blue UAS alternative' through Anzu channel.
gray_zone.action: Ground all Anzu Raptor airframes immediately; document purchase channel and compliance representations made at time of sale; prepare legal demand for refund or replacement citing DTPA violations and DJI hardware confirmation; do not accept firmware updates from Anzu pending litigation outcome.

sources.name: Texas AG Ken Paxton Lawsuit Filing

gray_zone.status: Subject to FCC Covered List; existing S1 ($359) and X1 ($599) inventory still available at retail despite prohibition on new FCC authorizations.
gray_zone.development: SkyRover app connects directly to DJI cloud infrastructure with identical encryption keys (confirmed by Jon Sawyer analysis). OTA update pipeline touches adversary infrastructure. Shell company filings trace to SZ Knowact Robot Technology Co Ltd. Gray zone risk score of 0.850 (CRITICAL) reflects firmware-level adversary control despite U.S. retail branding.
gray_zone.buyer_exposure: State and local buyers who purchased SkyRover S1/X1 for budget-constrained programs face data exfiltration risk via DJI cloud backend and inability to receive compliant firmware after January 1, 2027 FCC enforcement deadline.
gray_zone.action: Discontinue operation of SkyRover platforms on any network with access to sensitive data; isolate units to air-gapped test environments only; initiate procurement action for compliant replacement within 90 days; flag vendor relationship for fraud investigation if 'non-DJI' claims were made during sales process.

watch_list.why: 89% probability of operational cliff for covered list entities as firmware update prohibition takes effect, rendering existing inventory non-operational or non-compliant.
watch_list.trigger: FCC publishes final enforcement guidance 90 days before deadline (October 2026) or denies DJI/Autel reconsideration petitions.

watch_list.why: FAA-DoD safety agreement signed April 10, 2026 accelerates beam director and targeting subsystem procurement for border C-UAS applications; 88% probability of Q2-Q3 2026 acceleration.
watch_list.trigger: CBP or DoD issues RFP for production-scale HEL C-UAS systems citing April 10 FAA safety validation.

watch_list.why: 89% probability this figure is overstated due to double-count artifact (two flags with 99.9% identical parameters and only $100M variance).
watch_list.trigger: Budget reconciliation process or FOIA response clarifies whether this represents single program or aggregated multi-year projection.

predictions.event: Anzu Robotics product discontinuation due to adversary component shortage
predictions.hedge: Accelerate any pending Anzu Raptor replacement procurement to execute contract award by May 31, 2026; do not extend or renew Anzu service agreements beyond 60-day terms; shift budget allocation to Blue UAS List vendors with confirmed domestic or allied supply chains.

predictions.event: FCC Covered List firmware prohibition creates operational cliff for existing fleet inventory
predictions.hedge: Complete compliance audit of all airframes by June 30, 2026; allocate 20% of FY2027 UAS budget to emergency replacement procurement; pre-position contracts with Blue UAS vendors for rapid call-off orders in Q4 2026; plan for 30-60 day operational gap during fleet transition.

predictions.event: High-energy laser C-UAS border deployment accelerates beam director and targeting subsystem demand
predictions.hedge: Engage with HEL system integrators (BlueHalo, Epirus, Lockheed) now to lock pricing and delivery slots before border deployment contracts are awarded; budget for 12-18 month lead times on custom beam directors; coordinate with FAA on airspace deconfliction requirements per April 10 agreement framework.

signal_summary.top_concern: Component supply concentration risk meets regulatory enforcement cliff
